summary: PR 2541 reworked the baselib memory-card callback/function hsd_803A949C in hsd_3A94.c and updated its header prototype. The diff replaces local guessed command/state structs with a smaller command-buffer view and CMD_* access macros, switches helpers to the existing CardState type and file_info/digest fields, adds the chan parameter to hsd_803A949C, restructures several command cases, and includes a small polling loop around file_info.fileNo. The only comment evidence is the decomp bot report showing hsd_803A949C improved from 58.78% to 93.40%; no human review comments are present.

key_files.role: Main decompilation change for hsd_803A949C and local helper/types/macros.
Header prototype updated for hsd_803A949C to include chan.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: hsd_803A949C(s32 arg1) -> hsd_803A949C(s32 chan, s32 arg1)

what_changed.text: Changed extern hsd_804D7980 from s32 to volatile s32 in hsd_3A94.c.
Removed local CardStateExt and CardCmdEntry definitions and introduced CardBufEntry plus CMD_S32, CMD_STATE, CMD_PTR, and CMD_TYPE macros over hsd_804D1138/hsd_804D7980.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: added CardBufEntry and CMD_* macros

what_changed.text: Converted hsd_803A949C_Close and hsd_803A949C_IconSize to accept CardState* and use known fields such as file_info instead of the local extension field xC.
Changed hsd_803A949C from void hsd_803A949C(s32 arg1) to void hsd_803A949C(s32 chan, s32 arg1), and updated the matching header prototype.
Replaced entry->field accesses with CMD_S32/CMD_PTR/CMD_TYPE offset accesses throughout command handling.
Reworked some control flow to duplicate validation/copy checks inside slot-specific branches and to use direct byte-index access in case 13 instead of a CardBlock* helper path.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: duplicated block validation in CMD_S32(0x20)==0 and else branches

what_changed.text: Added a for loop up to 10 iterations waiting for state->file_info.fileNo to differ from -1 before assigning state->x20.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: for (i = 0; i < 10; i++) if (state->file_info.fileNo != -1) break

what_changed.text: The bot report recorded one unmatched-item improvement for main/sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3A94 hsd_803A949C from 58.78% to 93.40%.

smart_moves.text: Updating the public prototype in the owning header alongside the recovered function signature avoided leaving the signature change local to the C file.
Replacing the guessed CardStateExt wrapper with existing CardState field names improved type truth where the diff shows known fields such as file_info and digest.
The added small natural for loop around file_info.fileNo is a clearer source-level shape than manually repeated polling blocks, and is directly visible in the diff.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a recovered body proves that a callback/function receives an extra argument, update both the C definition and the owning header prototype in the same PR slice.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: hsd_803A949C(s32 chan, s32 arg1) in C
raw_diff: hsd_803A949C(s32 chan, s32 arg1) in header

decomp_lessons.lesson: Prefer existing recovered struct fields over one-off local extension structs when the known type can express the access; this PR moved CARDClose to state->file_info and digest accesses to CMD_STATE->digest.
Small source-level loops can be necessary matching structure even in low-level callback code; here a bounded 10-iteration loop replaced a direct fileNo read.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: for (i = 0; i < 10; i++) around state->file_info.fileNo

naming_conventions.text: New helper macros were named by command-buffer role and access type: CMD_S32(off), CMD_STATE, CMD_PTR(off), and CMD_TYPE.
The new function parameter name chan appears in both the C definition and header prototype, though the diff excerpt does not show it being used inside the body.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: void hsd_803A949C(s32 chan, s32 arg1)

naming_conventions.text: Local names such as icon_size, remaining, data_offset, hdr_plus_icon, slot, and i were used for command parsing and copy/compare calculations.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Used a volatile declaration for hsd_804D7980, presumably to affect or preserve load behavior; the diff proves the volatile change but not the author's exact intent.
Replaced an indexed CardCmdEntry pointer with offset-based command-buffer macros over hsd_804D1138 plus hsd_804D7980, a narrow matching tactic for command field accesses.
Restructured condition placement so CardBlock validation occurs inside each offset/non-offset branch rather than after a shared block pointer assignment.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: block validation moved into both branches

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Used direct byte-index expressions in case 13 instead of the hsd_803A949C_FileId helper/CardBlock pointer path for that case.

review_feedback.text: Automated report showed one improvement in an unmatched item: hsd_803A949C in main/sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3A94 improved from 58.78% to 93.40%, bytes +1679.
No human review comments or formal reviews are present in the loaded PR slice.

notes: Changed files, metadata, merge state, and bot score report are directly supported.
The diff excerpt was truncated in the loaded context, so an exact local read was used to capture the tail/header prototype change.
No PR body content and no human review comments were available.
The title says 92%, while the bot report says 93.40%; this record cites the bot report only as an automated report, not as independent validation.
Matching intent for volatile and macro shape is inferred only from code changes and should not be treated as reviewer-approved style without curator review.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Keep recovered signatures truthful across C and header
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2541 changed hsd_803A949C to take (s32 chan, s32 arg1) and updated hsd_3A94.h in the same change, supporting the existing truthful-header practice.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use known CardState fields instead of local guessed extension structs when available
accepted_candidate_records.text: This PR removed CardStateExt and used CardState fields such as file_info and digest in hsd_803A949C-related code.

source_update_candidates.title: hsd_3A94 contains baselib memory-card command/callback matching work
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2541 modified src/sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3A94.c and .h for hsd_803A949C, involving CARDFileInfo/CARDClose, command-buffer macros, CardState fields, and header prototype recovery.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful searchable path fact for future hsd_3A94/CardState/card command decomp work; should be curator-reviewed before promotion.

source_update_candidates.title: Investigate hsd_804D1138 command buffer offset semantics
source_update_candidates.text: The PR introduces CMD_S32/CMD_PTR/CMD_TYPE macros over hsd_804D1138 offsets 0x14, 0x18, 0x1c, 0x20, 0x24, 0x28, and 0x30. These may deserve a typed struct or documented offset map if later evidence confirms semantics.
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR supports that these offsets were used for matching, but names/field meanings remain mostly offset-based and should not be promoted without further source or callsite evidence.

rejection_notes: Do not promote volatile hsd_804D7980 as a general style rule; the PR proves only this declaration changed.
Do not claim hsd_803A949C fully matched; the loaded evidence only reports title '(92%)' and bot report 93.40%.
Do not infer human reviewer approval or rationale because no human review comments are loaded.
Do not promote CMD_* macros as preferred long-term API; they may be matching bridge code pending stronger type recovery.
